mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as indicated by consistent increases in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), signals a robust and expanding economy. This positive economic outlook boosts investor confidence, leading to increased demand for equities and driving S&P 500 forward returns higher.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings. This positive fundamental outlook for businesses drives equity valuations higher over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.1) in more than 50% of triggered instances of strong RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low, indicating a tight labor market, and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is simultaneously rising, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This combination typically precedes an economic slowdown or recession, negatively impacting equity markets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive (average return > 0.5%) during periods where both UNRATE is low and FEDFUNDS is rising, over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates renewed consumer and business confidence, along with increased investment. This surge in construction activity acts as a leading indicator for broader economic recovery and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) during post-recession periods when HOUST shows strong growth, in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low and stable inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, provides the Federal Reserve with flexibility to maintain accommodative monetary policies. This environment of low interest rates and stable prices supports higher equity valuations by reducing discount rates and fostering economic stability.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative (average return < -0.1%) during periods of stable or declining CPIAUCSL over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic activity. This indicates weakening demand and potential recessionary pressures, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive (average return > 0.5%) despite significant declines in INDPRO, in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is rising significantly while retail sales (RSAFS) are simultaneously declining, it indicates a severe deterioration in both labor market conditions and consumer demand. This combination signals a deep economic contraction, leading to substantial negative pressure on equity markets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND RSAFS declines > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive or flat (average return > 0%) when both UNRATE is rising and RSAFS is declining, over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms underlying corporate health and consumer strength. This fundamental economic vigor translates into sustained earnings growth expectations, driving positive momentum in the S&P 500 over the longer term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) during periods of confirmed strong GDPC1 growth, in more than 40% of triggered instances.
